questions.stem: What is the primary mechanism of action of penicillin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: inhibits bacterial RNA polymerase to block transcription

choices.label: B
choices.text: inhibits the 50S ribosomal subunit to block protein synthesis

choices.label: C
choices.text: inhibits DNA gyrase (topoisomerase) to block DNA replication

choices.label: D
choices.text: inhibits the transpeptidase that cross-links peptidoglycan in the cell wall

questions.explanation: penicillin inhibits the transpeptidase that cross-links peptidoglycan in the cell wall.

questions.stem: A tumor sample shows loss of function in a mismatch-repair gene. What is the most direct molecular consequence?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Conversion of DNA into RNA

choices.label: B
choices.text: Loss of the cell's plasma membrane

choices.label: C
choices.text: An increased rate of uncorrected replication errors (mutations)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Complete inability to transcribe any gene

questions.explanation: Mismatch repair corrects base-pairing errors missed by polymerase proofreading; its loss raises the mutation rate, a hallmark of certain hereditary cancers.

questions.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept item (text-only). Not real MCAT/AAMC content.

questions.stem: A newborn is temporarily protected against several infections by antibodies received across the placenta from the mother. This is an example of:

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: Active immunity from vaccination

choices.label: C
choices.text: Innate barrier defense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Passive immunity

questions.explanation: Antibodies transferred from another individual (here, mother to fetus) confer passive immunity, which is immediate but temporary because the recipient does not make the antibodies.

questions.stem: In biochemistry, what is a disaccharide?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a lipid made of glycerol and three fatty acids

choices.label: B

choices.label: C

choices.label: D

questions.stem: In biochemistry, what is hydrolysis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: a weak attraction between a hydrogen bonded to nitrogen or oxygen and another electronegative atom

choices.label: B
choices.text: the tendency of nonpolar groups to cluster away from water

choices.label: C

choices.label: D
choices.text: the breaking of a bond by adding water

questions.stem: In enzymology, what is the active site?

choices.label: B

choices.label: C
